meta.title: Oracle Time and Labor Mental Model
meta.sourceStatus: Content aligned to /docs/OTL-Core-Mental-Model.md as repository source of truth.
meta.validationNote: If an implementation detail is uncertain, mark it Needs review and verify against Oracle setup docs.

objects.name: HCM Groups
objects.definition: Plain English: audience selector for policy assignment. Oracle term: HCM Groups.
objects.purpose: Targets the right worker populations for entry and processing profiles.
commonMistakes: Worker qualifies for the wrong group and inherits incorrect policies.
Group criteria are too broad, causing mixed worker populations.

objects.name: Worker Time Entry Profile
objects.definition: Plain English: the front-door experience pack that controls how users enter time.
objects.purpose: Determines entry channels, interaction style, and which layout set appears.
commonMistakes: Profile allows the wrong entry method for a population.
Profile is linked to a layout that omits required fields.

objects.name: Layout Components, Categories, and Layout Sets
objects.definition: Plain English: screen architecture used to assemble reusable entry experiences.
objects.purpose: Builds the time entry UI by organizing fields/buttons for different worker groups.
commonMistakes: Too many one-off layouts instead of reusable components.
Layout set does not match the profile's intended reporting use cases.

objects.name: Time Attributes
objects.definition: Plain English: the data DNA that gives a time entry meaning (payroll type, project, absence, etc.).
objects.purpose: Classifies entered time so validation, calculation, and transfer rules can interpret it.
commonMistakes: Attributes are created but not exposed in fields/buttons.
Attribute values are inconsistent with payroll/project configuration.

objects.name: Time Card Fields
objects.definition: Plain English: UI wrappers that let people provide time attribute values.
objects.purpose: Collects structured input for validation and downstream processing.
commonMistakes: Required field not present on layout.
Field behavior does not match attribute intent (optional vs required).

objects.name: Worker Time Processing Profile
objects.definition: Plain English: processing brain that governs what happens after time is entered.
objects.purpose: Links workers to periods, consumer set, and rule/approval/transfer context.
commonMistakes: Wrong repeating period or consumer set mapped.
Processing profile not aligned with entry profile design.

objects.name: Repeating Time Periods
objects.definition: Plain English: the calendar spine for processing and approvals.
objects.purpose: Defines weekly/biweekly/etc cycles used by timecard and processing logic.
commonMistakes: Period boundaries misaligned with business/payroll cycle.
Multiple period patterns cause approval confusion.

objects.name: Time Entry Rule Sets
objects.definition: Plain English: policy checks that decide whether entered time can exist.
objects.purpose: Raises errors/warnings for invalid or non-compliant entries.
commonMistakes: Rule conditions miss edge cases like overlaps or required attributes.
Severity levels block users when warning was intended.

objects.name: Time Calculation Rule Sets
objects.definition: Plain English: derives business time outcomes from raw entries (overtime, premium, splits).
objects.purpose: Transforms entered time into payable/costable time interpretations.
commonMistakes: Rule execution order produces unexpected overtime results.
Thresholds or day-start settings do not match policy.

objects.name: Time Categories
objects.definition: Plain English: buckets that identify which entries belong to a processing or transfer context.
objects.purpose: Enables consumer sets and rules to target the right subset of time.
commonMistakes: Category includes too much or too little time.
Category not linked to consumer and rule logic consistently.

objects.name: Time Consumer Sets
objects.definition: Plain English: routing table that states which downstream systems consume time.
objects.purpose: Controls consumer-specific validation, approval periods, and transfer behavior.
commonMistakes: Wrong delivered set selected (Payroll Only vs Projects and Payroll).
Consumer rules configured but transfer process not scheduled.

objects.name: Approval Workflow
objects.definition: Plain English: reviewer chain that must bless time before transfer when required.
objects.purpose: Ensures managerial/project governance before downstream consumption.
commonMistakes: Approval routing falls back unexpectedly due to missing project manager context.
Time remains pending approval and never reaches transfer readiness.

objects.name: Transfer
objects.definition: Plain English: handoff of processed time to payroll, projects, or external systems.
objects.purpose: Moves approved/ready time from OTL repository to consuming applications.
commonMistakes: Transfer statuses not monitored after process runs.
Failures are diagnosed downstream without checking OTL readiness first.

lifecycleStages: Worker context exists (person, assignment, manager hierarchy)
Worker is linked through HCM group to entry and processing profiles
Time is captured (time card, calendar, web clock, or device import)
Calculation rules derive interpreted business time
Transfer sends time to payroll, projects, extracts, or external consumers
Downstream statuses and audit trail are updated
troubleshootingBranches: Confirm worker foundation (assignment, manager, payroll/project context)
Verify Worker Time Entry Profile and entry method
Verify layout set, fields, and time attributes visible to user
Verify Worker Time Processing Profile mapping
Check time categories and time consumer set routing
Review validation and calculation rule outcomes
Check approval state and approver routing
Inspect transfer readiness, process run, and error details

glossary.definition: Controls how time gets entered: channels, layout, and user interaction style.
Controls validation, calculation, approval context, and consumer routing.
Atomic meaning tags that classify time, such as payroll type, absence type, project, or task.
Fields and controls that expose attributes to users for entry.
Defines which consumer applications receive processed time and under what rules.
State where validations and required approvals are complete and handoff can proceed.

1. Who is the worker?.plainEnglish: Start with identity, assignment, and manager context.

3. Which Time Entry Profile?.plainEnglish: Verify the entry profile driving UI and methods.

3rd Party Device / Load.plainEnglish: Imported punches or events from external devices/files.

4. Which layout/fields/buttons appear?.plainEnglish: Check whether entry UI exposes required fields/buttons.

6. Which consumer set / time categories?.oracleTerm: Time Consumer Set and Time Categories

7. Which validation/calc rules fire?.plainEnglish: Inspect rules and resulting messages/derived time.

9. What transfer status/failure exists?.plainEnglish: Check transfer process results and errors.

Payroll.plainEnglish: Consumer that pays workers based on transferred time.

Payroll Transfer.plainEnglish: Transfer process path for payroll consumption.

Project Costing.plainEnglish: Consumer that costs labor against projects/tasks.

Project Costing Transfer.plainEnglish: Transfer process path for project costing consumption.

Project Execution Mgmt.plainEnglish: Consumer that uses labor for project execution/staffing.

Review validation and calculation rule o.oracleTerm: Review validation and calculation rule outcomes

Rules Engine.plainEnglish: Policy logic that checks and derives time outcomes.

Time Card.plainEnglish: Primary screen for entering and submitting work time.

Time Card Fields.plainEnglish: Screen fields used to capture attribute values.

Time Consumer Set.plainEnglish: Declares which downstream systems consume this time.

Time Entry Experience.plainEnglish: Where workers report time through configured channels.
Time Entry Experience.oracleTerm: Time card / calendar / web clock experience

Time becomes transferable.plainEnglish: Entry reaches ready state for consumer handoff.
